1	# Phase 7: Polish & Hardening — Implementation Specification
2	
3	**Goal:** Production-ready package with comprehensive edge case coverage, JSDoc documentation, polished error messages, and verified package configuration.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Task 1: JSDoc Comments on All Public API Surface
8	
9	### Scope
10	
11	Every exported function, method, class, interface, and type alias must have a JSDoc comment. This is the public API surface as defined in `src/index.ts`.
12	
13	### Files to modify
14	
15	1. **`src/core/tracker.ts`** — The `Tracker` class and its 16 public methods:
16	   - `constructor(cwd?: string)` — Explain cwd defaults to `process.cwd()`
17	   - `init()` — Creates `.trackgentic/` directory with all initial files
18	   - `create(params)` — Creates a new issue; describes all params with defaults
19	   - `list(params?)` — Lists issues from index with optional filters; describes sort order
20	   - `view(id)` — Returns full computed state by replaying events
21	   - `update(id, params)` — Updates issue fields; hierarchy/blockage side effects
22	   - `history(id)` — Returns raw event array for an issue
23	   - `commentsAdd(id, params)` — Adds a comment event
24	   - `commentsUpdate(id, commentId, params)` — Updates a comment
25	   - `commentsDelete(id, commentId, params?)` — Soft-deletes a comment
26	   - `commentsList(id)` — Returns computed comment list
27	   - `blockagesAdd(blockedId, params)` — Adds blockage(s); batch atomic, cycle detection
28	   - `blockagesResolve(blockedId, params)` — Resolves blockage(s)
29	   - `blockagesDelete(blockedId, params)` — Deletes blockage(s)
30	   - `blockagesList(id)` — Returns blockage info for an issue
31	   - `usersRegister(name)` — Registers user, returns token (no auth required)
32	   - `usersList()` — Lists users without tokens
33	   - `usersRevoke(name)` — Removes a user
34	   - `usersRegenerate(name)` — Regenerates own token only
35	
36	2. **`src/types/issue.ts`** — All exported types:
37	   - `IssueId` — "10-char string: 6 timestamp base36 + 4 random base36, sortable by creation time"
38	   - `CommentId` — "10-char string, same generation as IssueId"
39	   - `IssueStatus` — "Status progression: idea → todo → in-progress → done → closed"
40	   - `IssueProperties` — "Mutable properties of an issue"
41	   - `ComputedIssue` — "Full computed state including timestamps"
42	   - `ComputedComment` — "Computed state of a comment"
43	
44	3. **`src/types/event.ts`** — All exported event types:
45	   - `BaseEvent` — "Base shape shared by all events"
46	   - `CreationEvent` — "Marks issue birth"
47	   - `UpdateEvent` — "Records property changes; includes optional reason field for system events"
48	   - `CommentEvent` — "Adds a new comment"
49	   - `CommentUpdateEvent` — "Edits an existing comment"
50	   - `CommentDeleteEvent` — "Soft-deletes a comment"
51	   - `BlockageAddedEvent` — "Records new dependency"
52	   - `BlockageResolvedEvent` — "Records dependency resolution"
53	   - `BlockageDeletedEvent` — "Records dependency removal"
54	   - `Event` — "Union of all event types"
55	
56	4. **`src/types/index-file.ts`** — All exported types:
57	   - `IndexEntry` — "Summary of an issue for fast index lookup"
58	   - `IndexFile` — "Index structure: two sorted arrays + children map"
59	
60	5. **`src/types/dependency.ts`** — All exported types:
61	   - `BlockageEntry` — "Single blockage relationship between two issues"
62	   - `DependenciesFile` — "Bidirectional blockage maps (always in sync)"
63	   - `BlockageInfo` — "Blockage info for a specific issue (view output)"
64	
65	6. **`src/types/user.ts`** — All exported types:
66	   - `UserEntry` — "Registered user with token"
67	   - `UsersFile` — "Users file structure"
68	   - `UserInfo` — "Public user info without token"
69	
70	7. **`src/types/config.ts`** — `ConfigFile` interface
71	
72	8. **`src/types/api.ts`** — All request/response types (params interfaces and result types)
73	
74	9. **`src/core/errors.ts`** — `TrackgenticError` class and `ErrorCodes` constant
75	
76	### JSDoc style guide
77	
78	- Use `@param` for function/method parameters
79	- Use `@returns` for return values (describe the shape, not just "object")
80	- Use `@throws` for errors thrown (with error code)
81	- Use `@example` only for Tracker methods (skip for simple types)
82	- First sentence is a concise summary (no period needed for short ones)
83	- Follow with blank line and additional detail if needed
84	
85	### Example
86	
87	```typescript
88	/**
89	 * Create a new issue.
90	 *
91	 * Generates a unique ID, creates the issue file with creation + update events,
92	 * and inserts an entry into the sorted index.
93	 *
94	 * @param params - Creation parameters. `title` is required; all others have defaults.
95	 * @param params.title - The issue title
96	 * @param params.description - Optional description, defaults to ""
97	 * @param params.status - Initial status, defaults to "idea"
98	 * @param params.priority - Priority 1-5, defaults to 3
99	 * @param params.assignee - Optional assignee, defaults to null
100	 * @param params.tags - Optional tags, defaults to []
101	 * @param params.parentId - Optional parent issue ID for hierarchy
102	 * @param params.author - Override author (resolved by auth layer if not provided)
103	 * @returns `{ id }` on success, or a TrackgenticError on auth failure
104	 * @throws {TrackgenticError} NOT_INITIALIZED if no .trackgentic/ directory
105	 * @throws {TrackgenticError} NOT_FOUND if parentId doesn't exist in index
106	 * @throws {TrackgenticError} HIERARCHY_CONSTRAINT if parent is closed
107	 */
108	```
109	
110	### Exit criteria
111	
112	- `bun run docs:check` passes (TypeDoc parses all source without errors)
113	- Every export in `src/index.ts` has JSDoc on its declaration
114	- No `// TODO` or placeholder comments
115	
116	---
117	
118	## Task 2: Error Message Polish
119	
120	### Current state
121	
122	Error messages are functional but inconsistent. Some use periods, some don't. Some include the ID, others don't.
123	
124	### Requirements
125	
126	1. **Consistent format**: All error messages should follow the pattern:
127	   - `"Descriptive message ending with period."`
128	   - Use backticks around IDs: `` `abc123` ``
129	   - Include the operation context when possible
130	
131	2. **Review and fix all `throw new TrackgenticError(...)` calls in `tracker.ts`**:
132	   - NOT_INITIALIZED: `"No .trackgentic/ directory found. Run \`trackgentic init\` first."` (already good — verify consistency)
133	   - NOT_FOUND: `"Issue \`${id}\` not found in index."` (add backticks)
134	   - ISSUE_MISSING: `"Issue file for \`${id}\` is missing."` (add backticks)
135	   - COMMENT_NOT_FOUND: `"Comment \`${commentId}\` not found."` (add backticks)
136	   - HIERARCHY_CONSTRAINT: Keep existing messages from hierarchy.ts
137	   - BLOCKAGE_CYCLE: Add backticks around IDs
138	   - INVALID_PARAMS: Keep as is
139	   - Any others — review for consistency
140	
141	3. **Review `auth.ts` error messages**:
142	   - TOKEN_REQUIRED messages are good — verify consistency
143	   - INVALID_TOKEN: Keep as is
144	   - DEFAULT_USER_MISSING: Keep as is
145	
146	4. **Review `hierarchy.ts` error messages**:
147	   - `"Cannot add child to closed parent"` → consistent format
148	   - `"Cannot set parent to ${targetStatus}: child ${child.id} has status '${child.status}'"` → use backticks
149	
150	5. **No functional changes** — only message string updates. All tests should still pass without modification.
151	
152	### Exit criteria
153	
154	- All error messages end with a period
155	- All IDs in error messages are wrapped in backticks
156	- Consistent tone and format across all error paths
157	- All 528 existing tests still pass
158	
159	---
160	
161	## Task 3: Edge Case & Resilience Testing
162	
163	### New test file: `tests/integration/edge-cases.test.ts`
164	
165	Create integration-level tests that verify system resilience. These tests should use the Tracker class directly (not CLI) and test scenarios beyond the happy paths already covered.
166	
167	#### Test cases
168	
169	1. **Empty tracker operations**
170	   - `list()` on empty tracker returns `[]`
171	   - `view("nonexistent")` returns NOT_FOUND error
172	   - `history("nonexistent")` returns NOT_FOUND error
173	   - `commentsList("nonexistent")` returns NOT_FOUND error
174	   - `blockagesList("nonexistent")` returns NOT_FOUND error
175	   - `usersList()` on empty tracker returns `[]`
176	
177	2. **Invalid JSON in files**
178	   - Corrupt an issue file (write invalid JSON) → `view()` should throw a clear error (not a cryptic parse error)
179	   - Corrupt `index.json` → `list()` should throw a clear error
180	   - Corrupt `dependencies.json` → `blockagesList()` should throw a clear error
181	   - Corrupt `config.json` → any auth operation should throw a clear error
182	
183	3. **Missing files during operations**
184	   - Delete an issue file after it's in the index → `view()` returns ISSUE_MISSING
185	   - Delete an issue file after it's in the index → `update()` returns ISSUE_MISSING
186	   - Delete an issue file after it's in the index → `commentsAdd()` returns ISSUE_MISSING
187	   - (These are already partially tested — ensure comprehensive coverage)
188	
189	4. **Large number of events**
190	   - Create an issue, then apply 100+ update events → verify `view()` returns correct computed state
191	   - Create an issue with 50+ comments → verify `commentsList()` returns all correctly
192	   - Verify performance is reasonable (< 1 second for 100 events)
193	
194	5. **Self-referencing edge cases**
195	   - Create issue with itself as parentId → NOT_FOUND (ID doesn't exist yet) — this is already correct behavior
196	   - Add blockage where blockedId === blockerId → should be caught by cycle detection
197	   - Update an issue's status to its current status → no-op, no error (verify it works)
198	
199	6. **Concurrent write documentation**
200	   - Add a comment in the test file (not a test) documenting that concurrent writes are NOT safe and this is a known limitation documented in the architecture
201	
202	### Exit criteria
203	
204	- All edge case tests pass
205	- No changes to production code (only new test file)
206	- Total test count increases by at least 15 new tests
207	
208	---
209	
210	## Task 4: Package Configuration Verification
211	
212	### Requirements
213	
214	1. **Verify `package.json` fields**:
215	   - `name`: `"trackgentic"` ✓
216	   - `version`: `"0.1.0"` ✓
217	   - `type`: `"module"` ✓
218	   - `main`: Should be `"src/index.ts"` for Bun compatibility ✓
219	   - `types`: Should be `"src/index.ts"` ✓
220	   - `exports`: `{ ".": "./src/index.ts" }` ✓
221	   - `bin`: `{ "trackgentic": "./src/bin.ts" }` ✓
222	   - `files`: `["src/"]` ✓
223	   - Verify `description` field exists and is accurate
224	
225	2. **Verify CLI entry point works**:
226	   - Test that `bun run src/bin.ts --help` prints help text
227	   - Test that `bun run src/bin.ts init` creates `.trackgentic/` in cwd
228	   - Test that `bun run src/bin.ts create --title "Test"` creates an issue
229	
230	3. **Add a `prepublishOnly` script** to prevent broken publishes:
231	   ```json
232	   "prepublishOnly": "bun run quality"
233	   ```
234	
235	4. **Verify bin.ts has correct shebang** and is executable
236	
237	### New test file: `tests/integration/package.test.ts`
238	
239	- Test that the package.json has all required fields
240	- Test that the CLI entry point can be invoked with `bun run`
241	- Test that `--help` flag works
242	- Test that `--version` flag works (if implemented) or `--help` as fallback
243	
244	### Exit criteria
245	
246	- `package.json` has all required fields
247	- `prepublishOnly` script added
248	- CLI works from command line
249	- 3+ new package configuration tests pass
250	
251	---
252	
253	## Implementation Order
254	
255	1. **Task 2 first** (Error Message Polish) — Small, safe, no new files
256	2. **Task 1 second** (JSDoc Comments) — Large but mechanical, no functional changes
257	3. **Task 3 third** (Edge Case Testing) — New test file, no production changes
258	4. **Task 4 last** (Package Configuration) — Small changes to package.json, new test file
259	
260	After all tasks, run `bun run quality` and verify all gates pass.
261	
262	---
263	
264	## Final Exit Criteria (Phase 7 Complete)
265	
266	- All 528+ tests pass
267	- `bun run typecheck` — clean
268	- `bun run lint` — clean
269	- `bun run format:check` — clean
270	- `bun run docs:check` — clean
271	- JSDoc on all public exports
272	- Error messages consistent and polished
273	- Edge cases covered by tests
274	- Package configuration verified
275

1	/**
2	 * Typed error class for all trackgentic operations.
3	 * Each error has a result code, human-readable message, and CLI exit code.
4	 */
5	export class TrackgenticError extends Error {
6	  constructor(
7	    public readonly result: string,
8	    public override readonly message: string,
9	    public readonly exitCode: number,
10	  ) {
11	    super(message);
12	    this.name = "TrackgenticError";
13	  }
14	}
15	
16	// Error codes with their associated exit codes
17	export const ErrorCodes = {
18	  NOT_INITIALIZED: { result: "NOT_INITIALIZED", exitCode: 1 },
19	  ALREADY_INITIALIZED: { result: "ALREADY_INITIALIZED", exitCode: 0 },
20	  TOKEN_REQUIRED: { result: "TOKEN_REQUIRED", exitCode: 2 },

1	import { Command } from "commander";
2	import {
3	  blockagesAddAction,
4	  blockagesDeleteAction,
5	  blockagesListAction,
6	  blockagesResolveAction,
7	} from "./commands/blockages";
8	import {
9	  commentsAddAction,
10	  commentsDeleteAction,
11	  commentsListAction,
12	  commentsUpdateAction,
13	} from "./commands/comments";
14	import { createAction } from "./commands/create";
15	import { historyAction } from "./commands/history";
16	import { initAction } from "./commands/init";
17	import { listAction } from "./commands/list";
18	import { updateAction } from "./commands/update";
19	import {
20	  usersListAction,
21	  usersRegenerateAction,
22	  usersRegisterAction,
23	  usersRevokeAction,
24	} from "./commands/users";
25	import { viewAction } from "./commands/view";
26	
27	/**
28	 * Create and configure the Commander program.
29	 */
30	export function createProgram(): Command {
31	  const program = new Command();
32	
33	  program
34	    .name("trackgentic")
35	    .description("Issue tracker designed for AI agents — file-backed, event-sourced, git-friendly")
36	    .version("0.1.0");
37	
38	  // ─── init ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39	  program
40	    .command("init")
41	    .description("Initialize a new .trackgentic/ directory")
42	    .action(initAction);
43	
44	  // ─── create ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
45	  program
46	    .command("create <title>")
47	    .description("Create a new issue")
48	    .option("--description <string>", "Issue description")
49	    .option("--assignee <string>", "Assignee name")
50	    .option("--tags <comma-separated>", "Comma-separated tags")
51	    .option("--status <status>", 'Issue status (default: "idea")')
52	    .option("--priority <number>", "Priority 1-5 (default: 3)")
53	    .option("--parentId <id>", "Parent issue ID")
54	    .option("--path <string>", "Custom file path for the issue")
55	    .action(createAction);
56	
57	  // ─── list ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
58	  program
59	    .command("list")
60	    .description("List issues")
61	    .option(
62	      "--status <status>",
63	      'Filter by status (use "open" for non-closed, "closed" for closed)',
64	    )
65	    .option("--assignee <string>", "Filter by assignee")
66	    .option("--tags <comma-separated>", "Comma-separated tags (AND filter)")
67	    .option("--parentId <id>", 'Filter by parent ID (use "null" for top-level)')
68	    .action(listAction);
69	
70	  // ─── view ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
71	  program
72	    .command("view <issueId>")
73	    .description("View an issue's full computed state")
74	    .action(viewAction);
75	
76	  // ─── update ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77	  program
78	    .command("update <issueId>")
79	    .description("Update an existing issue")
80	    .option("--title <string>", "New title")
81	    .option("--description <string>", "New description")
82	    .option("--status <status>", "New status")
83	    .option("--assignee <string>", "New assignee")
84	    .option("--tags <comma-separated>", "New tags (replaces existing)")
85	    .option("--priority <number>", "New priority (1-5)")
86	    .option("--parentId <id>", 'New parent ID (use "null" to detach)')
87	    .action(updateAction);
88	
89	  // ─── history ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90	  program
91	    .command("history <issueId>")
92	    .description("View an issue's raw event history")
93	    .action(historyAction);
94	
95	  // ─── comments ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
96	  const commentsCmd = program.command("comments").description("Manage comments on issues");
97	
98	  commentsCmd
99	    .command("add <issueId>")
100	    .description("Add a comment to an issue")
101	    .requiredOption("--content <content>", "Comment content")
102	    .action(commentsAddAction);
103	
104	  commentsCmd
105	    .command("update <issueId> <commentId>")
106	    .description("Update an existing comment")
107	    .requiredOption("--content <content>", "New comment content")
108	    .action(commentsUpdateAction);
109	
110	  commentsCmd
111	    .command("delete <issueId> <commentId>")
112	    .description("Delete a comment")
113	    .action(commentsDeleteAction);
114	
115	  commentsCmd
116	    .command("list <issueId>")
117	    .description("List comments on an issue")
